titleStmt.title: Letter from Marie Martin to her mother, Mary Martin, 8 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Marie Martin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Marie Martin (1892-1975) to her mother Mary Lewis Martin (1866-1955). This letter seems to be incorrectly dated 8 July, but in it Marie refers to a letter she received from her mother dated 4 August. In the letter, Marie is responding to family news from her mother's last letter.Marie Helena Martin later known as 'Mother Mary', was a volunteer nurse during the First World War. She served in field hospitals in Malta, Leeds, and France working for the British army and later went on to become the founder of the Medical Missionaries of Mary (MMM).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
8-7-16 My very dearest Mother, A thousand thanks
for your very nice long latter dated
Aug 14 th which I received last
night, I was also delighted
to get the enclosed from
Tommy which I answer by
return post, I have been thinking
a great deal of him lately,
he must find the heat very
trying but I am so glad to
hear he is keeping so fit, it
is his only chance to escape
the fever â in a climate like
thatâ The heat has been
fair bad these last few
days but we are not very
busy just at present
I had such a nice long
letter from Minnie Fitzgerald to-day
when she wrote last time she
had not received my letter it
is very sad about T Waldron
I hope he will turn upâ his poor
young wife what an anxious
time she must be having
I am very sorry to hear about
Jack glands, it always rather
worries me to see them so bad
as it is very unnatural & he gets
them so oftenâ perhaps he ought
to go & see a specialist if the
change does not do him any
good. It is really very hard
to know what to do about
Jack I suppose Sr Ramsay
does not want to loose
him. Please give H. my very best
congratulation, he seems to
have done very well, how
I wish I were at home to
enjoy all the flowers, A Miss
Whitly come round very often
& brings us beautiful flowers
but is always reminds me
of homeâ Well I must close
this short note just to let
you know all is well with
me hoping you & all at
home are the sameâ Will
write long letter tomorrow Fondest love to allâ
yours ever Marie
